project.name: SIMS DMS
project.description: SIMS College of Pharmacy Discipline Management System — full-stack web app with React frontend, Express/Prisma backend, and Telegram bot integration

nodes.name: App.jsx
nodes.summary: Root React component — bootstraps MantineProvider, TanStack QueryClient, ToastProvider, ErrorBoundary, and BrowserRouter. AppRoutes sub-component reads the current user and redirects based on role (admin/faculty/super_admin) and must_change_password flag.

nodes.name: Breadcrumb.jsx
nodes.summary: Breadcrumb navigation component for page headers, using the `cn` utility for conditional class merging.

nodes.name: ComposeDrawer.jsx
nodes.summary: Slide-in drawer for composing and sending internal messages. Uses useMessages and useUsers hooks to fetch recipients and submit the message payload.

nodes.name: CreateUserDrawer.jsx
nodes.summary: Drawer form for creating new users (admin/faculty) with invite flow. Handles form state, validation, and API submission.

nodes.name: ErrorBoundary.jsx
nodes.summary: React class-based ErrorBoundary that catches render errors and displays a fallback UI to prevent full app crashes.

nodes.name: Layout.jsx
nodes.summary: Main application shell with responsive sidebar navigation, top header, and main content area. Role-aware — shows different nav links for admin, faculty, and super_admin users.

nodes.name: PWAUpdatePrompt.jsx
nodes.summary: Progressive Web App install/update banner that listens for service worker update events and prompts the user to reload for the latest version.

nodes.name: ProtectedRoute.jsx
nodes.summary: React Router v6 layout route wrapper that redirects unauthenticated users to /login and unauthorized users (role mismatch) back to their home route.

nodes.name: UploadStudentsDrawer.jsx
nodes.summary: Drawer for bulk-uploading students via Excel file. Validates file type, sends it to the server via useStudents hook, and shows row-level error feedback from the API response.

nodes.name: ViolationTypeDrawer.jsx
nodes.summary: Drawer form for creating and editing violation types (name, default fine, description). Delegates mutations to useViolationTypes hook.

nodes.name: Alert.jsx
nodes.summary: Inline alert component with severity variants (info, success, warning, error) for displaying contextual feedback within page content.

nodes.name: Badge.jsx
nodes.summary: Status badge/pill component with color variants driven by STATUS_COLORS and ROLE_COLORS constants for consistent visual representation of states and roles.

nodes.name: ConfirmDialog.jsx
nodes.summary: Modal confirmation dialog with a prompt message and confirm/cancel buttons, used for destructive actions throughout the app.

nodes.name: EmptyState.jsx
nodes.summary: Empty-state placeholder component with icon, heading, and optional sub-message for lists and tables with no data.

nodes.name: FormModal.jsx
nodes.summary: Mantine Modal wrapper pre-configured with a form layout, submit/cancel buttons, and loading state for inline data-entry modals.

nodes.name: Pagination.jsx
nodes.summary: Pagination control component with previous/next navigation, current page display, and total pages derived from props.

nodes.name: Skeleton.jsx
nodes.summary: Loading skeleton placeholder component with animated shimmer effect for table rows and card layouts while data is fetching.

nodes.name: StatCard.jsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard metric card displaying a title, numeric value, optional trend indicator, and icon for key statistics at a glance.

nodes.name: Table.jsx
nodes.summary: Reusable data table component with sortable columns, responsive horizontal scroll, and empty-state slot for structured data display.

nodes.name: Toast.jsx
nodes.summary: Toast notification system with ToastProvider context and useToast hook. Renders transient success/error/info messages with auto-dismiss using Mantine Notifications.

nodes.name: useAttendance.js
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hooks for attendance data: fetching live session attendance, fetching historical records, and manual override mutation — all backed by the shared axios api instance.

nodes.name: useAuth.js
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hooks for authentication: useCurrentUser (persists session via auth.js helpers), useLogin, useLogout, and useChangePassword mutations.

nodes.name: useCalendar.js
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hooks for the academic calendar: fetching calendar config, blocked dates, and mutations for updating working days, sessions per faculty, and blocked date management.

nodes.name: useCoverRequests.js
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hooks for duty-cover requests: listing, creating, claiming, approving, rejecting, and admin config mutations with cache invalidation.

nodes.name: useDebounce.js
nodes.summary: Simple debounce hook that delays updating the returned value until the specified delay has elapsed since the last change.

nodes.name: useDutySlots.js
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hooks for duty slot management: fetching available and assigned slots, faculty self-pick, admin-assign, and slot-swap mutations.

nodes.name: useInvites.js
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hooks for user invitation flow: creating invites, resending invite links, and regenerating tokens — mutations that invalidate the users query cache.

nodes.name: useMessages.js
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hooks for the internal messaging system: listing inbox/sent messages, sending a new message, and marking as read with optimistic cache updates.

nodes.name: useReports.js
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hooks for report generation: attendance summaries, violation counts, and faculty activity — parameterized by year/month filters.

nodes.name: useStudents.js
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hooks for student management: paginated search, Excel upload, promote, deactivate, and upload-log history — uses localStorage cache for repeated student lookups.

nodes.name: useUsers.js
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hooks for user administration: paginated user list, get by ID, update profile, deactivate, reactivate, and delete — with localStorage cache for user lookups.

nodes.name: useViolationTypes.js
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hooks for violation-type reference data: listing, creating, updating, deactivating, and deleting violation types used across the violations workflow.

nodes.name: useViolations.js
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hooks for the full violation lifecycle: listing, creating, hiding, flagging, resolving flags, and fetching audit logs — scoped to faculty or admin role context.

nodes.name: auth.js
nodes.summary: SessionStorage helpers for persisting the authenticated user object client-side: getUser, setUser, clearUser — used by useAuth hook for instant session restoration.

nodes.name: cache.js
nodes.summary: LocalStorage cache layer with per-entity TTL map (CACHE_TTL). Provides getCacheKey, setCacheKey, clearAllCache, and clearCacheKey functions used by useStudents and useUsers to reduce repeated API calls.

nodes.name: theme.js
nodes.summary: Theme management module with light/dark/system support. Exposes initializeTheme, setTheme, getTheme and manages class and data-theme attribute on the document root. Dark mode is currently force-disabled pending design review.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Single utility export: cn() — combines clsx and tailwind-merge for conditional Tailwind class string composition.

nodes.name: main.jsx
nodes.summary: React application entry point — mounts the App component with StrictMode, imports Mantine CSS, DM Sans/DM Mono web fonts, and the global stylesheet.

nodes.name: NotificationsPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Notifications inbox page displaying system and message notifications in a paginated table. Available to all authenticated users.

nodes.name: AdminDashboardPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Admin dashboard home page showing key statistics (users, students, cover requests, violations) as StatCards and a summary of today's attendance status.

nodes.name: AttendanceLivePage.jsx
nodes.summary: Real-time attendance monitoring page for admins showing today's duty session attendance, allowing manual override of attendance status for each faculty.

nodes.name: CalendarPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Academic calendar management page for configuring working days, blocking dates, setting sessions-per-faculty limits, and managing semester schedule.

nodes.name: CoverRequestsPage.jsx (admin)
nodes.summary: Admin view of all duty-cover requests with approval/rejection controls, filtering by status, and configuration of cover-request limits per slot.

nodes.name: DutySlotsPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Admin duty slot management page for viewing assigned/available slots by date, forcing slot assignments, and viewing the full duty roster.

nodes.name: ReportsPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Comprehensive reports page for admins with tabs for attendance reports, violation summaries by student, and faculty activity metrics — filterable by month/year with export capability.

nodes.name: StudentsPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Student roster management page with search, bulk Excel upload, pagination, year/semester promotion, and deactivation. Central hub for student record administration.

nodes.name: UsersPage.jsx
nodes.summary: User administration page listing all faculty/admin accounts with inline actions to invite, deactivate, reactivate, delete, and resend invitations. Opens CreateUserDrawer for new user creation.

nodes.name: ViolationTypesPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Reference data management page for violation types (name, default fine, active status). Opens ViolationTypeDrawer for create/edit operations.

nodes.name: ViolationsPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Admin violations review page with full violation list, flag/resolve workflow, and inline hide capability. Supports pagination and status filtering.

nodes.name: ChangePasswordPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Forced and voluntary password-change page with current/new/confirm password fields. Redirects to role-appropriate dashboard on success.

nodes.name: LoginPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Login page with email/password form and SIMS branding. Redirects to the appropriate role dashboard after successful authentication.

nodes.name: AttendancePage.jsx (faculty)
nodes.summary: Faculty self-service attendance view showing their scheduled duty slots and attendance status for the current month.

nodes.name: CoverRequestsPage.jsx (faculty)
nodes.summary: Faculty cover-request management page for viewing available cover opportunities, claiming uncovered slots, and tracking the status of their own requests.

nodes.name: DashboardPage.jsx (faculty)
nodes.summary: Faculty home dashboard showing upcoming duty slots, recent violations they filed, cover request summary, and unread message count as StatCards.

nodes.name: SlotPickerPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Faculty duty slot self-assignment page with a calendar-style grid of available slots by date and session. Faculty can pick open slots and view their assignments.

nodes.name: ViolationRecorderPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Faculty violation recording page for filing violations against students during their assigned duty slot. Requires selecting a student, violation type, fine amount, and optional photo.

nodes.name: MessagesPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Internal messaging page shared by all roles — displays inbox and sent tabs, opens ComposeDrawer for new messages, and shows message thread view.

nodes.name: AuditLogsPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Super-admin audit log page displaying all system actions (user changes, logins, violations) in a paginated table with actor, action, and timestamp.

nodes.name: SessionResetPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Super-admin tool for force-invalidating user sessions — lists all active sessions and allows targeted revocation for security incidents.

nodes.name: SuperAdminDashboardPage.jsx
nodes.summary: Super-admin dashboard with system-wide statistics across all departments and quick access links to audit logs and session management.

nodes.name: api.js
nodes.summary: Shared Axios instance pre-configured with credentials, base URL, CSRF token injection interceptor for unsafe methods, and automatic 401-to-login redirect on expired sessions.

nodes.name: constants.js
nodes.summary: Application-wide constants: ROLES enum, ROUTES path map, STATUS_COLORS and ROLE_COLORS maps for consistent Badge rendering across the app.

nodes.name: attendance.controller.js
nodes.summary: Express controller for attendance management: fetching live session attendance, historical records, and processing manual overrides with IST-aware time calculations and settings-driven thresholds.

nodes.name: auth.controller.js
nodes.summary: Authentication controller handling login (httpOnly cookie + CSRF token), logout (cookie clearing), and password change with bcrypt verification. Logs all auth events via audit service.

nodes.name: calendar.controller.js
nodes.summary: Calendar management controller: configuring academic year working days, managing blocked dates, setting sessions-per-faculty limits, and bulk duty-slot assignment with Telegram bot notifications.

nodes.name: cover-requests.controller.js
nodes.summary: Cover-request controller managing the full lifecycle: creating requests, volunteer claiming with atomic updateMany, admin approval/rejection, and config management. Uses Telegram for notifications and atomic SQL to prevent double-claiming.

nodes.name: duty-slots.controller.js
nodes.summary: Duty slot management controller: listing available/assigned slots, faculty self-pick, admin force-assign, and slot-swap operations with availability validation.

nodes.name: invites.controller.js
nodes.summary: User invitation controller: creating invite records, sending/resending invite links, and the Telegram bot activation flow using SELECT FOR UPDATE to prevent race conditions on invite claim.

nodes.name: students.controller.js
nodes.summary: Student management controller: paginated search, bulk Excel import (xlsx parsing), year/semester promotion, deactivation, and upload-log retrieval. Imports students from spreadsheet with error reporting.

nodes.name: users.controller.js
nodes.summary: User administration controller: listing, getting, updating profiles, deactivating, reactivating, deleting users, session management, and system settings CRUD — the most comprehensive controller in the backend.

nodes.name: violations.controller.js
nodes.summary: Violation lifecycle controller: creating violations (faculty), listing (admin/faculty), flagging for review, resolving flags, hiding violations, uploading photos, and retrieving per-violation audit logs.

nodes.name: bot.js
nodes.summary: Telegram bot handler using node-telegram-bot-api. Processes /start with invite tokens (SELECT FOR UPDATE to prevent race on claim), relinks existing accounts via /link command, and handles unknown commands.

nodes.name: cookieOptions.js
nodes.summary: Centralized cookie configuration factory returning httpOnly, sameSite, and secure options tuned for dev vs. production environments.

nodes.name: cron.js
nodes.summary: Scheduled task runner using node-cron. Runs nightly auto-checkout of faculty who didn't clock out, using IST time helpers and settings-driven thresholds.

nodes.name: logger.js
nodes.summary: Winston logger instance configured with console transport, ISO timestamps, and printf format. Used throughout the server for structured logging.

nodes.name: password.js
nodes.summary: Password utility module: bcrypt hash generation and comparison helpers, plus a random secure password generator used during invite activation.

nodes.name: prisma.js
nodes.summary: Singleton PrismaClient instance exported for use across all controllers and services. The central database access point for the entire backend.

nodes.name: telegram.js
nodes.summary: Telegram messaging helper that wraps sendMessage with graceful error handling — sends notifications to individual users via their stored Telegram chat ID.

nodes.name: time.js
nodes.summary: IST (Asia/Kolkata) timezone utility library providing toIST, getISTDate, getISTTimeString, parseISTTime, and isBeforeTime helpers. Critical for correct attendance and scheduling logic across the system.

nodes.name: bot.routes.js
nodes.summary: Express router for the Telegram webhook endpoint — verifies the secret token header and dispatches incoming updates to the bot handler.

nodes.name: backfill-passwords.mjs
nodes.summary: One-time migration script to generate and set bcrypt passwords for existing bot-activated users who were created before the password system was introduced.

nodes.name: audit.service.js
nodes.summary: Audit logging service providing a single logAction(actorId, action, metadata) function that writes to the AuditLog table — called fire-and-forget throughout all controllers.

nodes.name: settings.service.js
nodes.summary: System settings service: getSettings() fetches/initializes the singleton SystemSettings record with defaults, and startCronJobs() uses node-cron for scheduled tasks with settings-aware scheduling.

nodes.name: messages.controller.js
nodes.summary: Internal messaging controller: listing inbox/sent messages, sending a message to another user, and marking messages as read.

nodes.name: reports.controller.js
nodes.summary: Reporting controller generating attendance summaries, violation statistics by student, and faculty activity reports — all filtered by year/month with complex Prisma aggregation queries.

nodes.name: violation-types.controller.js
nodes.summary: CRUD controller for violation type reference data: listing active types, creating, updating, deactivating, and deleting violation type records.

nodes.name: index.js
nodes.summary: Express application entry point: configures middleware stack (CORS, helmet, cookie-parser, compression, rate limiting, JSON parsing, CSRF), mounts all route routers, registers the 404/error handlers, starts the HTTP server, and initializes cron jobs.

nodes.name: asyncHandler.js
nodes.summary: One-liner Express async error wrapper — wraps route handlers to catch async rejections and forward them to the next() error handler.

nodes.name: authenticate.js
nodes.summary: JWT-based authentication middleware: validates the httpOnly session cookie, looks up the user in Prisma, and attaches the user record to req.user.

nodes.name: authorize.js
nodes.summary: Role-based authorization middleware factory: returns a handler that checks req.user.role against allowed roles and 403s unauthorized requests.

nodes.name: csrf.js
nodes.summary: CSRF protection middleware using the double-submit cookie pattern: skips safe methods and unauthenticated requests, validates X-CSRF-Token header against the sims_csrf cookie.

nodes.name: validate.js
nodes.summary: Zod schema validation middleware for request bodies: parses and validates req.body against a provided schema, returning 400 with field errors on failure.

nodes.name: validateQuery.js
nodes.summary: Zod schema validation middleware for query parameters: validates req.query against a schema, coercing string values to typed numbers/booleans as needed.

nodes.name: admin.routes.js
nodes.summary: Express router for admin-only endpoints: system settings GET and PUT, restricted to super_admin role.

nodes.name: attendance.routes.js
nodes.summary: Express router for attendance endpoints: live session data (admin), historical records (all authenticated), and manual override (admin).

nodes.name: auth.routes.js
nodes.summary: Express router for authentication endpoints: POST /login, POST /logout, GET /me (current user), and POST /change-password.

nodes.name: calendar.routes.js
nodes.summary: Express router for academic calendar management: working days config, blocked dates CRUD, sessions-per-faculty limits, and bulk slot assignment — all admin-restricted.

nodes.name: cover-requests.routes.js
nodes.summary: Express router for the cover-request workflow: listing, creating (faculty), claiming, approving/rejecting (admin), and config management.

nodes.name: duty-slots.routes.js
nodes.summary: Express router for duty slot operations: available slots listing, faculty self-pick, admin force-assign, and slot-swap endpoints.

nodes.name: invites.routes.js
nodes.summary: Express router for user invitation management: creating invites, resending, regenerating tokens, and a public bot-activation endpoint.

nodes.name: messages.routes.js
nodes.summary: Express router for the internal messaging system: inbox listing, sent messages, sending a new message, and marking as read.

nodes.name: reports.routes.js
nodes.summary: Express router for report generation endpoints: attendance summary, violations by student, and faculty activity — admin-only with query param validation.

nodes.name: students.routes.js
nodes.summary: Defines Express routes for student management including Excel upload with multer, CSV upload-log retrieval, student search/list, promotion, and deactivation — all protected by authentication and role-based authorization.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses multer memory storage so uploaded Excel files are processed entirely in-buffer without touching disk; a dedicated 4-argument error handler intercepts multer errors before the global handler.

nodes.name: users.routes.js
nodes.summary: Declares Express routes for user CRUD operations (list, get, update profile, deactivate, reactivate, delete) with explicit 410 Gone stubs for deprecated POST/GET /users and regenerate-invite endpoints that now live under /invites.

nodes.name: violation-types.routes.js
nodes.summary: Registers Express routes for violation-type reference data (list, create, update, deactivate, delete) guarded by authentication and admin/super_admin authorization.

nodes.name: violations.routes.js
nodes.summary: Configures Express routes for the violations domain covering creation by faculty, admin listing, flag/resolve-flag workflow, hide, photo, and audit-log sub-resources — each route scoped to the appropriate role.

nodes.name: attendance.schema.js
nodes.summary: Exports a Zod schema (overrideSchema) that validates manual attendance override payloads, requiring at least one attendance field alongside a mandatory override reason.

nodes.name: auth.schema.js
nodes.summary: Exports Zod schemas for authentication — loginSchema validates email/password credentials and changePasswordSchema enforces minimum-length rules for password changes.

nodes.name: calendar.schema.js
nodes.summary: Provides Zod schemas for calendar management payloads: blocked dates, sessions-per-faculty limits, working-day arrays, and bulk slot assignment — all using a shared ISO date regex helper.

nodes.name: cover-requests.schema.js
nodes.summary: Exports Zod schemas for duty-cover request creation (duty slot UUID + optional reason) and admin cover-config updates (year, month, max-requests-per-slot limits).

nodes.name: duty-slots.schema.js
nodes.summary: Defines Zod schemas for duty-slot operations: faculty self-assignment (pickSlotSchema) and admin-forced assignment (adminAssignSchema), both requiring a valid ISO date and session type enum.

nodes.name: invites.schema.js
nodes.summary: Exports a Zod schema for creating user invitations, validating name, email, optional phone, role (admin or faculty), department, and designation fields.

nodes.name: messages.schema.js
nodes.summary: Exports a minimal Zod schema (sendMessageSchema) for validating internal message payloads with recipient UUID, subject, and non-empty body.

nodes.name: reports.schema.js
nodes.summary: Provides Zod query-param schemas for report endpoints: year/month filters, student-violation queries, faculty-activity queries, and active-student filters — all using z.coerce.number() to handle string query params.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses z.coerce.number() throughout to transparently convert URL query string values (always strings) to integers before validation.

nodes.name: settings.schema.js
nodes.summary: Exports a strict Zod schema for updating system-wide settings including late-threshold times, auto-checkout time, cover TTL, and session start times — uses .strict() to reject unknown keys.

nodes.name: students.schema.js
nodes.summary: Exports a Zod schema (promoteSchema) for validating student promotion payloads, requiring semester_or_year and an optional academic_year in YYYY-YY(YY) format.

nodes.name: users.schema.js
nodes.summary: Exports two Zod schemas: createUserSchema for full user creation with role/department/designation fields, and updateProfileSchema for partial self-service profile edits.

nodes.name: violation-types.schema.js
nodes.summary: Exports Zod schemas for violation-type management: createViolationTypeSchema requires name and non-negative default_fine, while updateViolationTypeSchema uses .refine() to ensure at least one optional field is present.

nodes.name: violations.schema.js
nodes.summary: Defines Zod schemas for the full violation lifecycle: creation (with student/slot/type UUIDs), editing (with at-least-one-field refinement), flagging with a note, and flag resolution with a reason.

nodes.name: csrf.test.mjs
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for the CSRF middleware, covering safe-method pass-through (GET/HEAD), unauthenticated request bypass, and token-mismatch/missing 403 rejection scenarios.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI pipeline running on push/PR to main. Installs dependencies, runs linting and server tests, ensuring code quality gates before merge.

nodes.name: SKILL.md (Design System)
nodes.summary: Master Claude Code skill definition for the SIMS DMS Design System. Documents brand guidelines, usage instructions, and component catalog for AI-assisted UI generation.

nodes.name: _adherence.oxlintrc.json
nodes.summary: OxLint rules configuration for design system adherence checks — enforces consistent use of DS tokens and components.

nodes.name: _ds_manifest.json
nodes.summary: Design system component manifest listing all exported components, their categories, and metadata for tool discovery.

nodes.name: readme.md (Design System)
nodes.summary: Developer-facing README for the SIMS DMS Design System skill, covering installation, usage, and quick-start examples.

nodes.name: styles.css (Design System)
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet for the design system providing base resets, utility classes, and component-level CSS built on the design token variables.

nodes.name: Avatar.prompt.md
nodes.summary: Usage guide and prompt instructions for the SIMS DMS Avatar design system component.

nodes.name: Badge.prompt.md
nodes.summary: Usage guide and prompt instructions for the SIMS DMS Badge component with color variants.

nodes.name: Button.prompt.md
nodes.summary: Usage guide and prompt instructions for the SIMS DMS Button component including all size and variant options.

nodes.name: Card.prompt.md
nodes.summary: Usage guide for the Card, CardHeader, and CardBody components in the SIMS DMS design system.

nodes.name: StatCard.prompt.md
nodes.summary: Usage guide for the StatCard dashboard metric component in the SIMS DMS design system.

nodes.name: Table.prompt.md
nodes.summary: Usage guide for the Table, Th, Td, Tr, and EmptyRow components in the SIMS DMS design system.

nodes.name: core.card.html
nodes.summary: HTML reference card showcasing all core SIMS DMS design system components (Avatar, Badge, Button, Card, StatCard, Table) with live rendered examples.

nodes.name: Input.prompt.md
nodes.summary: Usage guide for the SIMS DMS Input form component including label, placeholder, error state, and helper text.

nodes.name: Select.prompt.md
nodes.summary: Usage guide for the SIMS DMS Select dropdown component with option configuration and validation state.

nodes.name: forms.card.html
nodes.summary: HTML reference card for SIMS DMS form components (Input, Select) showing all states and validation patterns.

nodes.name: BrandMark.prompt.md
nodes.summary: Usage guide for the SIMS DMS BrandMark logo component including size variants and color options.

nodes.name: EmptyState.prompt.md
nodes.summary: Usage guide for the SIMS DMS EmptyState pattern component for displaying no-data placeholder states.

nodes.name: MobileCard.prompt.md
nodes.summary: Usage guide for the MobileCard pattern component optimized for touch interfaces in the faculty PWA.

nodes.name: SectionHeader.prompt.md
nodes.summary: Usage guide for the SectionHeader pattern component providing consistent page section titles.

nodes.name: patterns.card.html
nodes.summary: HTML reference card showcasing all SIMS DMS pattern components with live rendered examples.

nodes.name: brand-mark.card.html
nodes.summary: HTML reference card for SIMS DMS brand mark usage guidelines including logo variations, clear space, and misuse examples.

nodes.name: colors-brand.card.html
nodes.summary: HTML reference card for SIMS DMS primary brand color palette with hex values and usage guidelines.

nodes.name: colors-slate.card.html
nodes.summary: HTML reference card for the slate neutral color scale used in the SIMS DMS design system.

nodes.name: colors-status.card.html
nodes.summary: HTML reference card for semantic status colors (success, warning, error, info) used for feedback and state indication.

nodes.name: colors-surfaces.card.html
nodes.summary: HTML reference card for surface and background color tokens used in card and panel layering.

nodes.name: spacing-radius.card.html
nodes.summary: HTML reference card showing the SIMS DMS border-radius scale and usage guidance.

nodes.name: spacing-scale.card.html
nodes.summary: HTML reference card for the SIMS DMS spacing scale (4px base grid) with token names and pixel values.

nodes.name: spacing-shadows.card.html
nodes.summary: HTML reference card for elevation and shadow tokens used for card and dropdown depth hierarchy.

nodes.name: type-families.card.html
nodes.summary: HTML reference card for SIMS DMS typography families: DM Sans (UI text) and DM Mono (data/code) with weight specimens.

nodes.name: type-scale.card.html
nodes.summary: HTML reference card for the SIMS DMS typographic scale showing size, weight, and line-height for each text role.

nodes.name: base.css (tokens)
nodes.summary: Base CSS custom property tokens for the SIMS DMS design system — the root :root block defining all foundational variables consumed by components.

nodes.name: colors.css (tokens)
nodes.summary: Color design tokens as CSS custom properties: brand primary/secondary palette, status colors, and neutral slate scale.

nodes.name: fonts.css (tokens)
nodes.summary: Font family and weight design tokens — declares DM Sans and DM Mono as the primary UI and monospace typefaces.

nodes.name: spacing.css (tokens)
nodes.summary: Spacing design tokens using a 4px base grid — defines --space-1 through --space-16 and border-radius variables.

nodes.name: typography.css (tokens)
nodes.summary: Typography design tokens: font-size, line-height, and letter-spacing variables for the full text scale from xs to 4xl.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Root environment variable template listing all required server configuration: DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, SESSION_SECRET, CORS_ORIGIN, and NODE_ENV.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code project instructions pointing to CONSTITUTION.md as the single source of truth and referencing the active plan for context.

nodes.name: COMPLETE_FRONTEND_AUDIT.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive frontend audit document covering all React pages, components, and hooks for visual consistency and design system compliance.

nodes.name: CONSTITUTION.md
nodes.summary: Project constitution — the single source of truth defining SIMS DMS architecture, technology decisions, coding standards, and invariants. All development decisions defer to this document.

nodes.name: DEPLOYMENT_VERIFICATION_CHECKLIST.md
nodes.summary: Pre-deployment verification checklist covering environment config, database migrations, service connectivity, and smoke test procedures.

nodes.name: DESIGN_FIX_PROMPT.md
nodes.summary: Prompt template document for applying SIMS DMS design system fixes to UI components.

nodes.name: FEATURE_IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md
nodes.summary: Summary of completed feature implementations with status notes on what was built and remaining gaps.

nodes.name: FRONTEND_ARCHITECTURE_AUDIT.md
nodes.summary: Architecture-level audit of the React frontend covering component structure, data flow, and adherence to design patterns.

nodes.name: IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md
nodes.summary: High-level summary of the SIMS DMS implementation including what has been built and current system state.

nodes.name: MAJOR_FEATURES_PLAN.md
nodes.summary: Planned major feature roadmap for SIMS DMS covering upcoming enhancements to core domains.

nodes.name: MOBILE_DESIGN_RULES.md
nodes.summary: Mobile UI design rules specific to the SIMS faculty PWA including touch targets, layout constraints, and responsive breakpoints.

nodes.name: MOBILE_UI_FIXES.md
nodes.summary: Log of mobile UI issues identified and fixed in the faculty PWA with before/after descriptions.

layers.name: Frontend UI
layers.description: React pages, shared UI components, and the application shell that render the SIMS DMS interface for admin, faculty, super-admin, and auth flows.

layers.name: Frontend Data & Utilities
layers.description: TanStack Query data-fetching hooks, the Axios API client, auth helpers, client-side caching, theming utilities, and shared constants that wire the React UI to the backend.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Express route definitions, request validators, authentication/authorization middleware, and async error handling that form the HTTP API surface of the SIMS backend.

layers.name: Backend Service Layer
layers.description: Express controllers, domain services, shared server libraries (Prisma client, logger, Telegram bot, cron, password hashing), and data-migration scripts that implement SIMS business logic.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Prisma schema, SQL migrations, and seed scripts that define and populate the PostgreSQL database backing the SIMS discipline and user management system.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: Vitest server-side tests covering CSRF protection, authentication flows, Telegram bot logic, cron jobs, duty-slot rules, and invite flows.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Configuration
layers.description: CI/CD pipeline, Railway deployment config, root and workspace package manifests, environment templates, and all client/server build tooling that govern how SIMS is built and deployed.

layers.name: Design System & Dev Tooling
layers.description: The SIMS DMS Claude Code design-system skill (tokens, component specs, UI kit screens) and Spec Kit workflow tooling (.specify templates, scripts, and extensions) used during development.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project-level markdown docs covering architecture decisions, API references, database schema versions, Telegram integration guides, mobile design rules, feature specs, and operational runbooks for SIMS DMS.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: The README is the front door of SIMS DMS — it explains the system's purpose (discipline management for SIMS College of Pharmacy), the tech stack (React + Vite frontend, Express backend, PostgreSQL via Prisma, Telegram bot), and the three user roles: super_admin, admin (faculty coordinator), and faculty. Reading it first gives you the mental model you'll need to make sense of every file that follows.

tour.title: Database Schema
tour.description: The Prisma schema is the single source of truth for all data in SIMS DMS. It defines twelve interconnected models — User, Student, DutySlot, Violation, ViolationType, AttendanceRecord, CoverRequest, Message, Invite, AuditLog, SystemSettings, and their relations — that underpin every feature in the system. Understanding this schema before reading any code makes it immediately clear what each service and controller is operating on.
tour.languageLesson: Prisma uses a declarative schema language where models map to database tables. Relations are defined with @relation directives and generate type-safe query methods on the Prisma Client. The schema is the migration source: `prisma migrate dev` compares it to the current database state and generates SQL migration files.

tour.title: Server Entry Point
tour.description: server/index.js is where the Express application boots. It wires together the full middleware stack — CORS, Helmet security headers, cookie-parser, compression, rate limiting, JSON body parsing, CSRF protection — and then mounts every domain router under their respective URL prefixes. With fan-out of 18 (the highest on the server side), this file is the clearest map of the backend's surface area.
tour.languageLesson: Express middleware is applied in registration order. Security middleware (Helmet, rate limiting) should come before body parsers, which should come before route handlers. The logger is initialized first so it can capture every subsequent event during startup.

tour.title: Database Access & Core Libraries
tour.description: prisma.js exports a singleton PrismaClient used by every controller and service — it has the highest fan-in on the server (19 files import it), making it the foundation every database query depends on. The companion cron.js library uses node-cron to run a nightly auto-checkout job for faculty who forgot to clock out, demonstrating how scheduled server-side work integrates with the Prisma models from Step 2.
tour.languageLesson: The singleton pattern for PrismaClient prevents connection pool exhaustion in long-running Node.js servers. In development, Next.js/Express apps often attach the client to the global object to survive hot-module reloads without opening a new pool on each file change.

tour.title: API Middleware Stack
tour.description: Four middleware files form the protective layer in front of every route. asyncHandler wraps async route handlers to forward rejected promises to Express's error handler — without it, unhandled promise rejections would silently crash requests. authenticate validates the httpOnly JWT session cookie and attaches the resolved user to req.user. authorize is a middleware factory that gates routes to specific roles (super_admin, admin, faculty). validate applies Zod schemas to req.body, returning structured 400 errors before business logic ever runs.
tour.languageLesson: Express middleware factories (functions that return middleware functions) are a powerful pattern: `authorize('admin', 'super_admin')` returns a handler closure that captures the allowed roles array, keeping route definitions declarative. Zod's `safeParse` is preferred over `parse` in middleware because it returns a result object instead of throwing, giving you full control over the error response shape.

tour.title: Authentication Routes & Flow
tour.description: auth.routes.js defines the four endpoints that govern identity in SIMS DMS: POST /login (issue a JWT in an httpOnly cookie), POST /logout (clear it), GET /me (return the current user), and POST /change-password. These routes consume the authenticate and validate middleware from Step 5 and are the gateway that all other protected routes depend on. Understanding the auth flow here explains why the frontend's API client (seen in Step 9) automatically injects CSRF tokens.
tour.languageLesson: Storing JWTs in httpOnly cookies (instead of localStorage) protects against XSS attacks because JavaScript cannot read httpOnly cookies. The tradeoff is that cross-site request forgery (CSRF) becomes a concern, which is why SIMS DMS adds a CSRF token header for all state-changing requests.

tour.title: Core Business Domains: Violations
tour.description: Violations are the central domain of the discipline management system. The routes file registers endpoints for the full lifecycle — faculty creation, admin listing, flag-for-review, resolve-flag, and hide — each guarded by authenticate and role-specific authorize calls from Step 5. The controller implements the logic for each endpoint, including the audit trail that records every status change. The Zod schemas ensure that every mutation arrives with a valid student UUID, duty slot UUID, and violation type.

tour.title: Core Business Domains: Duty Slots & Attendance
tour.description: Duty slots define when faculty members are on duty, and attendance records track whether they clocked in and out. The duty-slot routes cover faculty self-pick, admin force-assign, and slot-swap operations — all with Zod validation. The attendance routes expose live session monitoring for admins and historical records for faculty, plus a manual override endpoint. Together these two domains connect the scheduling (who covers when) with the record-keeping (did they show up) that violations in Step 7 are filed against.

tour.title: Telegram Bot Integration
tour.description: The Telegram bot serves as the user onboarding and notification channel. bot.js handles the /start command with invite tokens using SELECT FOR UPDATE to prevent race conditions when multiple users activate the same link, and the /link command for re-linking an existing account. telegram.js is a thin helper that wraps sendMessage with graceful error handling so a failing Telegram notification never crashes a request. The bot routes register the webhook endpoint that Railway (Step 15) exposes to Telegram's servers.
tour.languageLesson: Webhooks are the push alternative to polling: instead of your server repeatedly asking Telegram 'any new messages?', Telegram POSTs each update to your HTTPS endpoint the moment it arrives. SELECT FOR UPDATE is a database-level advisory lock that prevents two concurrent requests from both marking the same invite token as used.

tour.title: Frontend Entry Point
tour.description: client/src/main.jsx is where the React application mounts. It renders the App component inside React StrictMode, imports the Mantine CSS reset and DM Sans/DM Mono web fonts, and registers the PWA service worker. This is the browser-facing counterpart to server/index.js from Step 3 — it boots the client runtime the same way the server boots the Express runtime.
tour.languageLesson: React StrictMode runs lifecycle methods and render functions twice in development to surface side effects caused by impure rendering. It has no production cost — the double-invoke is stripped in production builds. Registering the service worker here (before the app renders) ensures the browser installs the Workbox cache as early as possible.

tour.title: React Routing & Role-Based Access
tour.description: App.jsx is the most fan-out-heavy file in the entire codebase (fan-out: 30) — it imports every page component and wraps them in the providers that all pages depend on: MantineProvider for the design system, TanStack QueryClient for data fetching, ToastProvider for notifications, and ErrorBoundary for crash recovery. The AppRoutes sub-component reads the current user from context and redirects based on role and the must_change_password flag, implementing the client-side access control that mirrors the server's authorize middleware from Step 5.
tour.languageLesson: TanStack Query's QueryClient is placed high in the component tree so that all hooks (useQuery, useMutation) share the same cache instance. Placing providers in a single root component also ensures a single React context tree — nesting providers in individual pages would create separate cache islands.

tour.title: API Client & Shared Constants
tour.description: api.js is the shared Axios instance that every TanStack Query hook uses to reach the backend. It is pre-configured with credentials: true (so httpOnly cookies are sent cross-origin), the backend base URL from the environment, and a request interceptor that reads the CSRF cookie and injects the X-CSRF-Token header for all non-GET requests — closing the CSRF loop opened in Step 6. constants.js defines the ROLES enum, ROUTES path map, and the STATUS_COLORS/ROLE_COLORS lookup tables that are imported by 8 other files for consistent Badge and navigation rendering.
tour.languageLesson: Axios interceptors are middleware for HTTP requests/responses on the client side. A request interceptor runs before every outgoing call — ideal for injecting auth tokens or CSRF headers. A response interceptor can centrally handle 401s by redirecting to login, removing the need for error handling in every individual hook.

tour.title: Application Shell & UI Primitives
tour.description: Layout.jsx is the most-imported file in the entire frontend (fan-in: 20) — it renders the responsive sidebar, top header, and main content area, and is role-aware so admin and faculty see different navigation items. The three UI primitives imported almost as widely are Badge (fan-in: 16), Table (fan-in: 13), and Toast (fan-in: 18): Badge renders color-coded status and role pills using the constants from Step 12, Table provides sortable columns with empty-state support for all data views, and Toast is the notification system that confirms every mutation.
tour.languageLesson: High fan-in components are the load-bearing walls of a React codebase. Changing Layout, Badge, or Table ripples through nearly every page — which is why SIMS DMS keeps them in a dedicated ui/ directory and driven entirely by props and constants, with no internal data fetching. This separation of shell/display from data-fetching keeps them easy to test and refactor.

tour.title: Data Hooks & Feature Pages
tour.description: The TanStack Query hooks (useViolations, useDutySlots, useAttendance) are the data layer that connects the Axios client from Step 12 to the React pages. Each hook encapsulates a set of related queries and mutations, manages cache invalidation, and exposes loading/error states — so pages stay focused on rendering. The three corresponding pages (ViolationsPage for admin review, ViolationRecorderPage for faculty filing, AdminDashboardPage for the overview) show how these hooks are consumed: the admin dashboard aggregates statistics across all three domains in a single StatCard grid, while domain-specific pages render the Table primitives from Step 13 populated with the hook's data.
tour.languageLesson: TanStack Query's queryKey arrays are the cache keys. Hooks like useViolations use a queryKey of ['violations', filters] so that changing any filter parameter automatically triggers a fresh fetch without manual cache invalidation. useMutation's onSuccess callback calls queryClient.invalidateQueries to keep list views in sync after a create or update.

tour.title: CI/CD & Deployment
tour.description: The GitHub Actions CI pipeline (ci.yml) runs on every push and pull request to main: it installs dependencies, runs the linter, and executes the Vitest server-side test suite to gate merges. railway.toml configures the production deployment on Railway.app — specifying the build command, start command, and health-check path that Railway uses to verify the server is live after each deploy. Together these two files close the loop from code change to running production system.
tour.languageLesson: Railway uses a Nixpacks buildpack by default but respects a railway.toml for custom build and start commands. The healthcheckPath is polled after deploy — if it does not return 200 within the timeout, Railway rolls back to the previous deployment automatically, providing zero-downtime deploy safety.
